channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Poll: In the midst of a tight presidential race, Trump draws support from voters disillusioned with democracy
item.description: New polling shows this year’s Harris v. Trump presidential race is too close to call, with Trump drawing support from voters who are disillusioned with democracy. Results from the McCourtney Institute for Democracy’s Sept. 23-27, 2024 Mood of the Nation Poll.
item.dc:creator: Eric Plutzer and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: Presidents position selves as 'savior' to acquire power: O'Reilly
item.description: O'Reilly argues that Trump is seen as a savior by his base due to his direct approach to their fears about cultural and national changes.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: All about the politics of disaster relief
item.description: Republicans are castigating the Biden administration for its response to Hurricane Helene – and, like rote, are bringing to the forefront the ugly politics of disaster response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters pitch encampment outside Jewish Democrat's Ohio home
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Masked pro-Palestinian demonstrators escalated their harassment of a Jewish congressman on the eve of Monday’s anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, pitching an encampment outside Ohio Democratic Rep. Greg Landsman ’s Cincinnati residence.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MAGA candidate vying to flip Virginia Senate seat red stands firm on controversial military comments
item.description: Republican Hung Cao, vying for Virginia's Senate seat, believes he can flip it red and stands by his controversial remarks on military training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit accuses state department, DOJ of stonewalling FOIA requests regarding climate office personnel
item.description: Power the Future filed a against a Biden Administration climate office alleges the department is dodging congressional oversight
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Springfield, Ohio, not the only small town facing immigrant influxes
item.description: Several rural and small towns say initial challenges with immigrants is a small price to pay for future prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer: Oct. 7 'will go down in infamy'
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.), the highest-ranking Jewish elected leader in American history, marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks in Israel as a “day that will go down in infamy” and pledged America “will never forget.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear appeal from Elon Musk’s X platform over warrant in Trump case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Monday it won’t hear an appeal from the social media platform X over a search warrant prosecutors obtained in the election-interference case against former President Donald Trump. The justices did not explain their reasoning and there were no noted dissents.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma death row inmate had three 'last meals.' He's back at Supreme Court in new bid for freedom
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma has set execution dates nine times for death row inmate Richard Glossip. The state has fed him three “last meals.” Glossip has even been married twice while awaiting execution.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan and Georgia Arab American voters sour on Harris
item.description: Vice President Harris is facing growing signs that Arab American and Muslim voters are souring on her in the key battlegrounds of Michigan and Georgia as anger rises over the expanding conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court declines Biden's appeal in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday that a court order finding Texas hospitals cannot federally be required to provide pregnancy terminations when they violate a state abortion ban will stay for now.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lake rips Biden-Harris 'double whammy' policies affecting Arizonans : 'Driven us over the cliff'
item.description: AZ GOP Sen. candidate spoke to Fox News Digital about how inflation and illegal immigration have disproportionally hurt Arizonans over the past four years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP New Jersey Senate candidate Curtis Bashaw nearly passes out during debate
item.description: New Jersey Republican Senate candidate Curtis Bashaw appeared to freeze on Sunday during his debate against Democrat opponent Rep. Andy Kim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Republicans mark Oct 7 attack 1 year out as Israel-Hamas war continues
item.description: Senate Republicans issued a renewed condemnation of the Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attacks perpetrated by Hamas in Israel one year after they occurred.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP looks to avoid disaster in Nebraska Senate race
item.description: Republicans are looking to avoid a disaster in the Nebraska Senate race amid signs that an independent candidate could be giving incumbent Sen. Deb Fischer (R) a run for her money.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: The “Fox News Sunday” interview reflected a broader media blitz by presidential nominee Kamala Harris and Walz as the Democrats seek to garner public attention in the final 30 days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Plot to Kill Trump: Back to Butler
item.description: Former President Donald Trump returned to Butler, Pennsylvania, three months after surviving an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pledges ongoing federal support as she visits North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris pledged ongoing federal support and praised the “heroes among us” as she visited North Carolina on Saturday in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, her second trip in four days to the disaster zone.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton projected to reach Category 4 strength before landfall in Florida
item.description: A new storm is approaching the west coast of the Florida, threatening to inflict heavy rain, strong winds and flash flooding in the already-storm battered state.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Florida voters concerned about election disruptions after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Polling sites along the coast have lost locations due to Hurricane Helene's damage, and Florida voters are starting to worry about how this could change things for the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Craig, Teirab tangle over Social Security, abortion in first head-to-head 2nd District debate
item.description: In a policy driven debate, candidates in Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District part on fiscal and abortion policy while finding common ground on response to Jan. 6 Capitol uprising.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Biden says he is worried about violence around the presidential election
item.description: President Biden told reporters he was confident the election “will be free and fair,” before saying, “I don’t know whether it will be peaceful.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: The ruling came one day before the temporary restraining order would expire, allowing the administration to continue the program while the case is moved to Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kamala Harris is making an unusual pilgrimage today — to the birthplace of the GOP
item.description: Harris will travel Thursday to Ripon, Wisconsin — home to the Little White Schoolhouse, where an 1854 meeting led to the birth of the Republican Party. She'll be joined by former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, WGN 9

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9
